extra.message: unserialize() called without the allowed_classes option set to false (or a whitelist). Without this restriction, all classes available in the autoloader may be instantiated, enabling Object Injection attacks. Use unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false]).

unserialize() called without the allowed_classes option set to false (or a whitelist). Without this restriction, all classes available in the autoloader may be instantiated, enabling Object Injection attacks. Use unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false]).

openssl_random_pseudo_bytes() without checking the $crypto_strong output parameter may return non-cryptographically-strong bytes on some systems. Always check the second argument or prefer random_bytes() which throws on failure.
md5() used for hashing data. If this value is used for authentication, integrity verification, or token generation, md5 is insufficient. Use a stronger algorithm.
md5() used for hashing data. If this value is used for authentication, integrity verification, or token generation, md5 is insufficient. Use a stronger algorithm.
md5() used for hashing data. If this value is used for authentication, integrity verification, or token generation, md5 is insufficient. Use a stronger algorithm.
wp_redirect() does not restrict the redirect target to the current host; consider using wp_safe_redirect() to prevent open redirect vulnerabilities when the URL may be influenced by user input.
rand() is not cryptographically secure and must not be used to generate security tokens, nonces, or passwords. Use wp_generate_password() or random_bytes() instead.
rand() is not cryptographically secure and must not be used to generate security tokens, nonces, or passwords. Use wp_generate_password() or random_bytes() instead.
Passing a variable directly to $wpdb query methods without prepare() may allow SQL injection. Use $wpdb->prepare() to safely parameterize queries.

REST route registered without a permission_callback. This defaults to open access in older WordPress versions. Always define a permission_callback that verifies user capabilities.
